studies.risk_class: Risk Class I according to MDR
studies.duration_additional_info: The training programme lasts for 8 weeks. Pre-post training comparisons are calculated immediately after the training and 3 months later.
studies.intervention_and_control_group: Intervention group (IG) and wait-list control group (CG)
studies.additional_info_about_trial_arms: Intervention group receives an 8-week psychotherapy-based online program to treat fatigue (ELEVIDA). Wait-list control group receives the training after the study is completed.
studies.collected_data: Chalder Fatigue Scale, Fatigue-Scale-Motor-Cognition (FSMC), Hospital Anxiety and Depression Scale (HADS), Hamburg Quality of Life Questionnaire in MS (HAQUAMS), Multiple Sclerosis Neuropsychological Screening Questionnaire (MSNQ), Cognitive and behavioural responses to symptoms questionnaire, Brief Illness Perception Questionnaire, Coping Self Efficacy Scale (CSES), Frenchay Activity Index, Revised NEO Personality Inventory, MRI connectivity measures (DTI and resting state fMRI).

studies.risk_class: Risk Class I according to MDR
studies.duration_additional_info: Outcomes were assessed at baseline, at week 12 (postintervention) and at follow-up (week 24).
studies.intervention_and_control_group: Intervention (ELEVIDA) n=139; Waitlist control group n=136
studies.additional_info_about_trial_arms: The intervention group received ELEVIDA, a 12-week self-guided, interactive, online fatigue management programme based on principles of cognitive behavioural therapy (CBT) and mindfulness. The control group was a waitlist control.
studies.collected_data: Chalder Fatigue Scale (primary outcome), patient determined disease steps (PDDS), self-reported fatigue.
studies.key_outcomes_findings: Compared with the control group, significantly greater reductions in Chalder Fatigue Scale scores were seen in the ELEVIDA group at week 12 (primary endpoint, intention-to-treat analysis: between-group mean difference 2.74 points; 95% CI 1.16 to 4.32; p=0.0007; effect size d=0.53). These effects were sustained at week 24 (intention-to-treat analysis: between-group mean difference 2.19 points; 95% CI 0.57 to 3.82; p=0.0080).

audit.study_title: Online therapy programme for fatigue in multiple sclerosis
audit.note: registry-only: outcome measures captured under collected_data; no reported results

audit.study_title: Randomised controlled trial of a self-guided online fatigue intervention in multiple sclerosis.
